creators.bio: The 7 Figure Fitness Mentor. Helping online fitness coaches scale to 6-7 figures. Instagram Authority Funnel creator. DM 'GROW' to get started.

topContent.caption:
If you've given up on Facebook ads for your fitness business... you're not alone.

But here's what changed for 3 coaches last month:

Coach 1: $0 → $8K/mo in 47 days
Coach 2: Went from 3 clients to 22 in 60 days
Coach 3: First $15K month after 2 years of trying

The difference? They stopped boosting posts and started running a real Instagram Authority Funnel.

DM 'ADS' if you want me to map out your funnel for free.
topContent.hookFramework: Empathy Open → Pain Validation → Social Proof Stack (3 case studies) → Proprietary System Name → Free Offer CTA
topContent.visualHook: Bold white text overlay: 'GIVEN UP ON ADS?' with red background flash
topContent.textHook: If you've given up on Facebook ads for your fitness business...
topContent.spokenHook: If you've given up on Facebook ads, you're not alone — but don't quit yet
topContent.summary: Validates frustration with ads, then reframes failure as wrong approach. Stacks 3 specific case studies with dollar amounts. Positions proprietary system as the fix.

topContent.caption:
Stop creating content. Start creating conversations.

Slide 1: The old way — post and pray
Slide 2: Why algorithms hate 'value content'
Slide 3: The DM Trigger Framework
Slide 4: How to turn 1 post into 15 DMs
Slide 5: Real results from last week

Save this and try it on your next post.

topContent.textHook: Stop creating content. Start creating conversations.
topContent.summary: Challenges conventional content wisdom. Introduces DM Trigger Framework as alternative to posting value content. Drives saves.

topContent.caption:
I spent $127 on this ad and made $4,200 in 9 days.

Here's the exact breakdown:
- $127 ad spend
- 47 leads generated
- 12 DM conversations
- 3 new clients at $1,400/each

That's a 33x return.

The secret? I didn't send them to a landing page. I sent them to my Instagram profile.

Want me to show you how? DM 'FUNNEL'
topContent.hookFramework: Specific Numbers Hook → Step-by-Step Breakdown → Contrarian Reveal (no landing page) → Curiosity CTA
topContent.visualHook: Calculator graphic showing $127 → $4,200 with green arrow
topContent.textHook: I spent $127 on this ad and made $4,200 in 9 days.
topContent.spokenHook: One hundred twenty seven dollars turned into four thousand two hundred in nine days
topContent.summary: Uses specific, verifiable numbers to build authority. The 33x return is attention-grabbing. Contrarian twist of sending to profile instead of landing page drives curiosity.

creators.bio: Swedish entrepreneur. Teaching how to make money with online courses using Skool, YouTube, ads & AI. Free Skool community: skool.com/max

topContent.caption:
Meta's Andromeda update changed everything.

Broad targeting now BEATS lookalike audiences 90% of the time.

Here's what to do:
1. Kill your lookalikes
2. Go broad (age + country only)
3. Let Andromeda's AI find your buyers
4. Focus your budget on creative testing

I've tested this across 40+ accounts. The data doesn't lie.
topContent.hookFramework: News Hook (algorithm change) → Contrarian Data Point → 4-Step Action Plan → Authority Close

topContent.spokenHook: If you're still using lookalike audiences, you need to hear this
topContent.summary: Positions as early adopter of Meta's algorithm changes. Specific, actionable advice. The '40+ accounts' proof point builds credibility.

topContent.caption:
I spend $200/day on ads. Here's my entire tech stack:

- Meta Ads Manager (obviously)
- Skool for community ($99/mo)
- Stripe for payments (free)
- Cal.com for booking (free)
- Canva for creatives ($12/mo)
- Google Sheets for tracking (free)

Total monthly cost: $111/mo

You don't need ClickFunnels. You don't need Kajabi. You don't need a $500/mo tech stack.

Simple wins.
topContent.hookFramework: Transparency Hook (real spend) → Full Stack Reveal → Cost Breakdown → Anti-Complexity Message

topContent.textHook: I spend $200/day on ads. Here's my entire tech stack:

topContent.summary: Transparency about spend builds trust. The shockingly low tech cost ($111/mo) validates the anti-complexity message. Great for coaches overwhelmed by tools.

topContent.caption: Here's how I added $41,513/month in 27 days using Skool + YouTube + Ads. Step by step breakdown of the exact funnel.

creators.bio: Skool Games Winner. Teaching coaches to build profitable communities. From $0 to $100K/mo with Skool. Free training in bio.

topContent.caption:
I went from $0 to $100K/mo in 8 months using Skool.

No ads. No sales calls. No fancy funnels.

Just a Skool community + workshops + DM conversations.

Here's the exact playbook:
1. Free community (deliver real value)
2. Weekly workshops (create demand)
3. DM conversations (close in chat)
4. Stack paid tiers ($100 → $1K → $5K)

This model works because you're building relationships, not running campaigns.

Want the full breakdown? Comment 'SKOOL'
topContent.hookFramework: Result Hook → Anti-Method List → Simple System → 4-Step Playbook → Relationship Positioning → Comment CTA
topContent.visualHook: Skool dashboard showing revenue, scrolling to $100K number
topContent.textHook: I went from $0 to $100K/mo in 8 months using Skool.
topContent.spokenHook: Zero to a hundred thousand a month in eight months. No ads, no sales calls, no funnels.
topContent.summary: The anti-ads, anti-funnel message directly targets Dan's audience. 4-step playbook is simple and repeatable. Comment CTA drives engagement.

topContent.caption:
The Skool Games taught me this:

The coaches who win aren't the loudest. They're the most consistent.

Slide 1: Post daily in your community
Slide 2: Run weekly workshops (not monthly)
Slide 3: DM every new member within 24 hours
Slide 4: Make your free tier so good people feel guilty not paying
Slide 5: The money follows the trust

Which one of these are you NOT doing?

topContent.textHook: The Skool Games taught me this:
topContent.summary: Leverages Skool Games credential. Each slide is one tactical action. The closing question drives comments and self-reflection.

topContent.caption:
Stop trying to get 1,000 members in your Skool community.

You only need 30 paying members at $100/mo to make $3K/mo.

Or 30 members at $300/mo to make $9K/mo.

Or 30 VIPs at $1,000/mo to make $30K/mo.

Same 30 people. Different offer.

Focus on depth, not width.
topContent.hookFramework: Contrarian Command → Math Breakdown (3 tiers) → Reframe (same people, different offer) → Philosophy Close
topContent.visualHook: Calculator animation: 30 × $100, 30 × $300, 30 × $1,000
topContent.textHook: Stop trying to get 1,000 members in your Skool community.
topContent.spokenHook: You don't need a thousand members. You need thirty.
topContent.summary: Simple math that challenges the 'more members' mindset. The tier comparison shows the same 30 people can be worth $3K or $30K. Directly relevant to Dan's model.

creators.bio: We help shorten sales cycles with YouTube. Pre-align prospects. Reduce education. Increase closes. CEO of Scaling With Systems. 3,000+ clients served.

topContent.caption:
Every coach I know making $50K+/mo does these 3 things:

1. They DON'T do sales calls (they close in DMs or applications)
2. They charge $3K+ minimum (low-ticket is a trap)
3. They run ONE funnel, not five

The simplicity is the strategy.

Most coaches fail because they're running 5 half-built systems instead of 1 great one.

Which of these 3 is your biggest gap? Comment below.
topContent.hookFramework: Pattern Hook (successful coaches) → 3 Contrarian Behaviors → Simplicity Thesis → Self-Assessment CTA
topContent.visualHook: Split screen: 'Coaches at $10K' vs 'Coaches at $50K+' list
topContent.textHook: Every coach I know making $50K+/mo does these 3 things:
topContent.spokenHook: Every single coach I know who's making fifty K or more per month does these three things
topContent.summary: Positions no-call selling, high pricing, and simplicity as the 3 keys. Directly aligns with Dan's Close By Chat methodology. Drives comments via self-assessment.
topContent.cta: Which is your biggest gap? Comment below.

topContent.caption:
I replaced my entire sales team with a chatbot and my revenue went UP.

Here's what happened:
- Removed 4 sales reps ($24K/mo in payroll)
- Built an AI chat qualifier + application funnel
- Close rate went from 22% to 38%
- Monthly revenue increased 15%

People don't want to be 'sold.' They want to be heard and guided.

AI does that better than most sales reps.

DM 'AI' if you want to see how it works.
topContent.hookFramework: Shock Statement → Data Comparison (before/after) → Philosophy Shift → Offer CTA
topContent.visualHook: Screenshot of chat dashboard with 'Sales Team: FIRED' overlay
topContent.textHook: I replaced my entire sales team with a chatbot and my revenue went UP.
topContent.spokenHook: I fired my entire sales team and replaced them with AI and my revenue went up
topContent.summary: Controversial hook drives engagement. Specific numbers ($24K saved, 38% close rate) make it credible. The AI angle is timely and aligns with the no-phone-sales trend.

topContent.caption:
The $100K/mo coaching business model on one page:

1. ONE offer at $3K-$5K
2. ONE traffic source (paid ads)
3. ONE conversion mechanism (application → DM → close)
4. ONE delivery method (group coaching + Skool)
5. ONE team member (VA at $1K/mo)

Revenue: $100K/mo
Expenses: ~$25K/mo (ads + VA + tools)
Profit: $75K/mo

This is the model. Stop overcomplicating it.
topContent.hookFramework: Promise Hook (on one page) → 5 ONEs Framework → Math Proof → Simplicity Command

topContent.textHook: The $100K/mo coaching business model on one page:
topContent.summary: The 'ONE' framework is memorable and shareable. Simple math proves the model. Directly mirrors Dan's approach (group coaching, Skool, DMs, no phone).
